scope_description: Review and fix the Codex repeated empty subagent-wait problem shown in the supplied screenshot; explicit capped cell plus frozen feature-close working-tree artifacts.

included.reason: Capped behavior-change implementation with RED/GREEN evidence.
Immutable content identity for uncommitted feature history, spec, backlog, badge, and learning artifacts; no A10 cell preflight implied for this commit-only entry.

excluded.reason: Dropped before feature wording changed after .agents write denial; blocker report is evidence only.
Separate active feature; excluded from the frozen hash.

head: working-tree-sha256:111504718f1f4e5a7a59c12248da966f97fab1673d025adc0fc9f721478c81a6

reviewer_manifest.runtime_constraint: Four total collaboration slots require 3+2 dispatch while preserving the same frozen scope.

findings.summary: Live root-only Codex deployment is not behaviorally proven.
findings.today: The current Codex boundary relies on root AGENTS.md because .agents is sandbox-read-only, but the GREEN replay also loaded canonical procedure files.
findings.why: The replay proves the combined future payload, not that root AGENTS.md alone overrides the stale live skill projection.
findings.scenario: A precedence or wording regression makes root doctrine ineffective while canonical procedure wording still drives the expected answer, yielding a false GREEN.
findings.fix: Replay all frozen scenarios with a fresh child loading only root AGENTS.md, record verbatim output, and require the exact D3 sequence in all three cases.

findings.summary: Shipped wording does not explicitly lock D3's at-least-one minimum.
findings.today: Live surfaces say to continue material task-local work when any remains, without defining when the progress interval is satisfied.
findings.why: The locked decision requires at least one material non-wait action before a later wait.
findings.scenario: Different orchestrators either re-wait after one useful action or postpone result collection until every independent check finishes.
findings.fix: Use 'perform at least one material task-local action when work remains' across doctrine, procedures, spec, projections, and census.

findings.summary: The procedure census permits contradictory timeout instructions to pass.
findings.today: Procedure surfaces only need scattered keywords and do not inherit all no-exception, loophole, interruption, and external-polling assertions.
findings.why: A later procedure can retain those tokens while adding an urgency or no-chatter exception.
findings.scenario: Swarming guidance adds 'under deadline pressure, immediately re-wait'; the census remains green and repeated empty panels return.
findings.fix: Apply the full doctrine assertions to every bounded procedure section, including no exceptions, loopholes, interruption, and external polling.

findings.summary: The cell is capped as verified even though its exact stored verify command exits 1.
findings.today: The cell records verify_passed=true for `node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && git diff --check`, while a fresh execution returns exit 1 with empty output; only the two constituents were observed green separately.
findings.why: Capping requires the exact runnable verify command to pass with proof. Marking a failing literal command as passed makes the review preflight and merge evidence untrustworthy.
findings.scenario: A later review treats the capped cell as verified, even though the command encoded in the cell cannot demonstrate success in the current environment.
findings.evidence: .bee/cells/codex-agent-wait-loop-2.json: verify, trace.verification_evidence, trace.verify_output, trace.verify_passed; fresh review execution on 2026-07-15: exit 1, empty stdout/stderr
findings.fix: Do not treat constituent success as success of a failing stored conjunction. Reopen/repair verification state or rerun the exact command in a child-process-capable environment and record its real passing output before approval.

findings.summary: No test executes the native wait sequence the feature claims to fix.
findings.today: The GREEN proof is a model answering an A/B/C questionnaire and is forbidden from calling further tools.
findings.why: It proves rule comprehension, not live tool-selection behavior after a real timeout.
findings.scenario: Codex explains the right sequence in replay but still emits wait_agent → timeout → wait_agent in an actual delegated gather.
findings.fix: Add controlled native traces for ordinary delegation and swarming that force one empty timeout and assert the full progress/commentary/later-wait sequence without interrupt, redispatch, or ownership release.

findings.summary: The doctrine census can pass contradictory D3 ordering.
findings.today: Assertions check independent keywords across whole files but never tie material work/list snapshot, commentary, and later wait into the required order.
findings.why: A surface can contain every required token while explicitly placing the later wait before commentary.
findings.scenario: A mutation stating 'later bounded wait may run before commentary' satisfies the current predicates and keeps the regression suite green.
findings.fix: Validate a localized normalized contract block with order-sensitive assertions and mutation rows for D1-D5 inversions.

findings.summary: GREEN covers only the no-local-work fallback and scores one ambiguous scenario as exhausted.
findings.today: All three GREEN outcomes choose one list_agents snapshot; none requires doing material local work, and scenario 3 omits the exhaustion precondition used by the rubric.
findings.why: The other half of D3 is unproved and an always-snapshot behavior would still pass.
findings.scenario: Useful report synthesis remains after a timeout, but the orchestrator snapshots agent status instead; current GREEN evidence endorses it.
findings.fix: Add a frozen material-work-remains scenario, clarify scenario 3, and cover zero-agent plus completion-during-interval traces.

findings.summary: Completion events can satisfy the interval without being handled.
findings.today: Shipped doctrine requires material work or list_agents then commentary, but does not carry the locked condition that a completion event counts only when its result is handled.
findings.why: A completion can arrive during local work and leave the next commentary and wait based on stale agent state.
findings.scenario: wait_agent times out; an agent completes during unrelated local work; the orchestrator reports it as running and waits again without consuming the completed report.
findings.fix: Carry the event-handling condition into doctrine/procedures and anchor a completion-during-interval case that is processed exactly once before another wait.

findings.summary: Safety anchors can pass after ownership protections are inverted.
findings.today: The prose forbids interrupt, duplicate dispatch, claim release, and reservation release, but whole-file keyword checks do not pin negation or the external-process boundary on every procedure.
findings.why: A future edit can invert 'never licenses' to 'licenses' while retaining every token.
findings.scenario: A timeout instruction begins canceling and redispatching quiet workers and releasing ownership while the census remains green.
findings.fix: Assert localized normalized safety clauses with explicit negation and external carve-out on every surface; add mutation cases for redispatch, ownership release, stale completion, and external executor timeout.

decision.summary: Independent five-angle review found one unresolved P1: the exact cell verify command exits 1 although the capped trace records verify_passed=true. Seven deduplicated P2 groups require repair or stronger proof. No merge approval until P1 is replaced by literal passing evidence and the repair delta is re-reviewed.
decision.next_action: Open a separate repair scope for gated-auto findings, produce root-only and native trace evidence, then delta re-review; rerun exact verification in a capable environment.
